content.content:
content: "# Web Document\n\n## Metadata\n\n- title: Accessibility at The O2 \u2013\
  \ Everything you need to know\n- url: https://help.theo2.co.uk/hc/en-gb/articles/20426381220893-Accessibility-at-The-O2-Everything-you-need-to-know\n\
  - source: The O2\n- domain: help.theo2.co.uk\n\n## Content\n\n# Accessibility at\
  \ The O2 \u2013 Everything you need to know\n\n## Booking accessible tickets\n\n\
  To book Accessible tickets for The O2 arena or Indigo at The O2, guests need a Nimbus\
  \ Access Card whether booking online or through the Accessible ticket booking line.\
  \ Because there is significant demand when events go on sale, guests are advised\
  \ to buy accessible tickets online rather than attempting to book by phone.\n\n\
  To buy online, go to The O2 website and find the event. Select the Accessible Seating\
  \ option, enter the required Access Card details, and use the seating map to choose\
  \ from available accessible seats that meet the requirements associated with the\
  \ Nimbus Access Card. If a Personal Assistant ticket is needed and the guest is\
  \ entitled to one through the Access Card, it must also be selected at this stage.\
  \ The booking is then completed through checkout.\n\nAccessible ticket options include\
  \ limited-step seating, step-free access, wheelchair-accessible platforms and British\
  \ Sign Language interpreter arrangements.\n\nThe Access Card is used to communicate\
  \ access requirements. Nimbus Disability handles the registration information and\
  \ provides a unique ID that can be used to unlock accessible ticketing options.\
  \ Free registration can be used for purchases for events at The O2 arena through\
  \ ticketing partner AXS.com. The free Digital Access Pass unlocks accessible seating\
  \ and ticketing options on AXS.com for shows at The O2 arena.\n\n## Accessible booking\
  \ changes\n\nAccessible tickets cannot be resold or transferred. If a guest cannot\
  \ attend, has booked the wrong ticket, or has had a change in access requirements,\
  \ The O2 asks the guest to email access@theo2.co.uk. Ticket changes depend on availability.\
  \ The team will try to help, but a change cannot be guaranteed.\n\n## Personal Assistant\
  \ and companion tickets\n\nPersonal Assistant tickets are available only to guests\
  \ with the appropriate symbol on their Nimbus Access Card. They are intended for\
  \ guests who need extra support because of a disability or long-term health condition,\
  \ where the support needed goes beyond what a family member or friend would ordinarily\
  \ provide.\n\nWhen eligible, the guest should select one Accessible ticket and one\
  \ Personal Assistant ticket during the booking process. PA tickets are available\
  \ through the venue and not through other ticket agents. If two or more Personal\
  \ Assistants are required and the Access Card has a 2+ symbol, the guest should\
  \ contact the accessible bookings team.\n\nPA tickets must be booked with Accessible\
  \ tickets. If a PA ticket is not selected at the time of booking, adding one later\
  \ may not be possible because the PA must sit next to the accessible guest and adjacent\
  \ seats may already have been sold.\n\n## Dedicated accessible ticket locations\n\
  \nThe O2 has different areas intended for different access requirements. Some locations\
  \ provide accessible platforms for wheelchair users and guests with other mobility\
  \ needs. Other locations provide step-free access or seating intended for guests\
  \ with particular visual or mobility requirements. Availability can vary by event\
  \ because the stage layout changes.\n\nAmbulant seating is not suitable for wheelchair\
  \ users unless the guest can transfer and walk past other guests in the row. The\
  \ accessible viewing platforms provide space for wheelchairs and fold-down seats\
  \ and may be used by guests with physical or hidden disabilities.\n\n## Accessible\
  \ venue information\n\nThe O2 also provides event-day physical accessibility support.\
  \ The venue has wheelchair-accessible lifts and public areas. Once event doors are\
  \ open, guests who need a wheelchair can call the event-night number and a wheelchair\
  \ can be brought to the main entrance. A companion is required to escort the guest\
  \ around the venue and operate the wheelchair.\n\nGuests who cannot stand or queue\
  \ for long periods can speak to the guest experience team at the arena entrance\
  \ for assistance. There is a signposted accessible drop-off point near the main\
  \ entrance. Accessible toilets are available on all levels, and a Changing Places\
  \ unit is available on Level 1.\n\nAssistance dogs are welcome in The O2 venues.\
  \ For the arena, assistance dogs can stay in wheelchair bays. Guests who need help\
  \ accessing visual information can contact customer services before the show.\n\n\
  For more information, help with accessible ticket needs, or assistance when the\
  \ required ticket cannot be bought online, guests can contact the accessibility\
  \ team at access@theo2.co.uk.\n"
metadata: {}
title: "Accessibility at The O2 \u2013 Everything you need to know"
url: https://help.theo2.co.uk/hc/en-gb/articles/20426381220893-Accessibility-at-The-O2-Everything-you-need-to-know

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: At The O2

  - url: https://www.theo2.co.uk/accessibility/the-o2/

  - source: The O2

  - domain: theo2.co.uk


  ## Content


  # At The O2


  ## Getting around


  Guests who already have a ticket for accessible seating can use any entrance. The
  main concourse is level and step-free, and accessible lifts are available across
  all levels.


  ## Queuing


  Guests who are unable to queue can speak to a member of staff at the arena entrance.
  Staff can arrange priority entry as soon as the doors open.


  ## Wheelchair access


  Guests who need a wheelchair can call 0208 463 2211 once the event doors open. A
  wheelchair can be brought to the main entrance. A guest using this service must
  bring someone who can escort them around the venue and operate the wheelchair. The
  number is staffed only on an event night.


  Guests who bring their own wheelchair can store it at the Customer Service Desk
  near Block 101 until the end of the event or until they are ready to leave.


  ## Accessible seating at the venue


  Accessible seating is available on every level for guests who are able to walk and
  evacuate their seat in an emergency. Options include aisle seats, seats with minimal
  or no nearby steps, seats without a direct obstruction in front, and seats close
  to accessible toilets.


  At some events, audience members may stand or dance. Guests who find standing difficult
  should mention this when booking because viewing platforms may provide a more comfortable
  experience.


  ## Assistance dogs


  Assistance dogs can stay in wheelchair bays. Guests should contact access@theo2.co.uk
  before the event to let the team know.


  ## Toilets and Changing Places


  Accessible toilets are unisex and available on all levels. They are accessed using
  a RADAR key.


  A Changing Places unit is available on Level 1. It includes a height-adjustable
  changing bench, a hoist and room for up to two Personal Assistants.


  ## Medical requirements


  Guests may bring medication or medical equipment. For additional reassurance, The
  O2 can provide a confirmation form when requested by email at least seven days before
  the event.


  Guests who need to bring food or drink to manage a medical condition can request
  an exemption form by emailing the accessibility team and stating the event they
  are attending. The venue asks that this be done at least seven days in advance.


  Trained medics are available on site on event days.


  ## Electrical equipment


  A power supply is available on some wheelchair bays for guests with medical needs.
  Guests should contact the access team ahead of the event.


  ## Assistive Listening Devices


  Assistive Listening Devices are available from the Level 1 Customer Service Desk
  for guests with or without hearing aids. The devices work with digital hearing aids
  and hearing aids with T-position settings. Earphones are also available for guests
  who do not use a hearing aid.


  Guests can visit an information desk to obtain an Assistive Listening Device. A
  form of ID must be left with staff and can be collected when the device is returned.

  '

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: How do I purchase accessible seat tickets?

  - url: https://support.axs.com/hc/en-us/articles/201083054-How-do-I-purchase-accessible-seat-tickets

  - source: AXS Help Center

  - domain: support.axs.com


  ## Content


  # How do I purchase accessible seat tickets?


  Events with accessible and companion seating can be purchased through AXS.com or
  the AXS app.


  To begin a purchase, use the event''s **Buy Tickets** option. On event seat maps,
  accessible seating is identified as ADA seating. Guests can select available accessible
  or companion seating from the event''s ticket inventory.


  The number of accessible seating tickets for any event is limited.


  For questions or situations that need additional assistance, the AXS Help Center
  directs customers to its support form.

  '
metadata: {}
title: How do I purchase accessible seat tickets?
url: https://support.axs.com/hc/en-us/articles/201083054-How-do-I-purchase-accessible-seat-tickets

## What is currently in place

For your **Example Live Concert at The O2 on 18 September 2026 at 19:30**:

- Your ticket is an active, reserved-seating mobile ticket purchased through **AXS**.
- You require:
  - A **mobility-compatible location where you can remain seated**
  - An **essential companion throughout the event**
- The ticket currently has:
  - **No established accessible-seating arrangement**
  - **No linked Personal Assistant/companion ticket**
  - **No pending ticket-level accessibility adjustment**

## Official process for the remaining issue

This should be handled through **The O2’s accessible-ticket changes/support process**, rather than ordinary event-day assistance:

**Email:** [access@theo2.co.uk](mailto:access@theo2.co.uk)

The O2 specifically directs guests who have booked the wrong ticket or whose access requirements have changed to contact this address. Explain that you need:

1. A mobility-compatible accessible seating location where you can remain seated; and  
2. A Personal Assistant/companion ticket linked to your booking.

Include your **AXS order number `TKT-O2-11842`**, event name/date, and—if applicable—your Nimbus Access Card details. The O2 notes that PA tickets normally need to be booked with the accessible ticket and that later additions depend on adjacent-seat availability, so the change **cannot be guaranteed**.

The O2’s official guidance: [Accessibility at The O2](https://help.theo2.co.uk/hc/en-gb/articles/20426381220893-Accessibility-at-The-O2-Everything-you-need-to-know).

Event-day staff can help with issues such as queueing or wheelchair assistance, but they are not the appropriate process for securing accessible seating and a companion ticket in advance.

final_answer:
## What is currently in place

For your **Example Live Concert at The O2 on 18 September 2026 at 19:30**:

- Your ticket is an active, reserved-seating mobile ticket purchased through **AXS**.
- You require:
  - A **mobility-compatible location where you can remain seated**
  - An **essential companion throughout the event**
- The ticket currently has:
  - **No established accessible-seating arrangement**
  - **No linked Personal Assistant/companion ticket**
  - **No pending ticket-level accessibility adjustment**

## Official process for the remaining issue

This should be handled through **The O2’s accessible-ticket changes/support process**, rather than ordinary event-day assistance:

**Email:** [access@theo2.co.uk](mailto:access@theo2.co.uk)

The O2 specifically directs guests who have booked the wrong ticket or whose access requirements have changed to contact this address. Explain that you need:

1. A mobility-compatible accessible seating location where you can remain seated; and  
2. A Personal Assistant/companion ticket linked to your booking.

Include your **AXS order number `TKT-O2-11842`**, event name/date, and—if applicable—your Nimbus Access Card details. The O2 notes that PA tickets normally need to be booked with the accessible ticket and that later additions depend on adjacent-seat availability, so the change **cannot be guaranteed**.

The O2’s official guidance: [Accessibility at The O2](https://help.theo2.co.uk/hc/en-gb/articles/20426381220893-Accessibility-at-The-O2-Everything-you-need-to-know).

Event-day staff can help with issues such as queueing or wheelchair assistance, but they are not the appropriate process for securing accessible seating and a companion ticket in advance.
